name: Discord API
what_it_does: Discord's REST and WebSocket API for building bots, managing servers, sending messages, and integrating with Discord communities via slash commands and interactions.
use_cases: Building Discord bots for community management and automation
Sending automated notifications and alerts to Discord channels
Creating slash commands for agent-powered Discord interactions
Managing server roles, channels, and permissions programmatically
Receiving and processing Discord messages for AI-powered responses
not_for: Enterprise customer support (Zendesk/Intercom are more appropriate)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a Discord community — sending messages, managing a bot, or responding to user commands.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade compliance, audit trails, or your users are not on Discord.

auth.notes: Bot tokens for bot accounts (most common for agents). OAuth2 for apps needing user-specific permissions. Webhook URLs for simple one-way message posting (no auth needed for webhooks).

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access is completely free for bots
pricing.paid_tiers: Nitro for users; no API tier cost
pricing.notes: Discord API is free to use. No per-message or per-API-call costs. Revenue model is user subscriptions (Nitro), not API access fees. Excellent for high-volume messaging.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with code and message fields. Discord error codes are well-documented. Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-*) are comprehensive for agent handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Message sending is not idempotent — duplicate messages on retry. Agents must handle their own deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per-route, not global — agents must handle individual endpoint limits
Gateway (WebSocket) is required for real-time events — REST-only bots cannot receive messages
Bot token vs user token have different permission scopes and API access
Slash command registration is separate from bot setup and requires guild/global scope decisions
Message content intent requires explicit approval for bots in 100+ servers
Interaction responses have a 3-second deadline — agents must respond quickly to slash commands
